subject: Science
chapter: The Invisible Living World: Beyond Our Naked Eye
source_pdf: 2.The Invisible Living World Beyond Our Naked Eye.pdf

key_points: Microorganisms are tiny living organisms that cannot be seen with the naked eye
Microscopes help scientists observe cells and microorganisms
A cell is the basic unit of life
Cells have three main parts: cell membrane, cytoplasm, and nucleus
Microorganisms play important roles in decomposition, food production, and agriculture
examples: Yeast is used in bread making
Rhizobium bacteria fix nitrogen in plant roots
Microalgae produce oxygen in aquatic ecosystems
formulas_or_processes: Fermentation: Microorganisms convert sugars into gases or acids
Nitrogen fixation: Atmospheric nitrogen → usable nitrogen compounds
Photosynthesis in microalgae: Carbon dioxide + water → oxygen + food
exam_tips: Remember that the cell is the basic structural and functional unit of life
Know the difference between plant cells and animal cells
Understand the role of microorganisms in daily life and ecosystems
Revise important scientists such as Robert Hooke and Antonie van Leeuwenhoek
